text:
There was a young fox who loved pancakes. Every weekend, after breakfast, he would disappear into his woods to bake them. One lazy Saturday morning, before slumping onto his cozy bed, he set the kettle dancing and stirred his favorite batter.

The pancake monster was out!

He leaped out into the tree canopy only to realize something was terribly amiss. The sky had turned into a swirling cauldron, rain pouring down in thick drops. Then, from the swirling mess, pancakes emerged!

First came fluffy stacks, golden brown and adorned with vibrant berries. Then came pancakes with swirls that looked like whimsical whirlwinds. Last were the chocolate chip monsters, bursting with pieces of brownies.

The fox, momentarily mesmerized by their charm, let out a whoop. He dove into the puddle near the pancakes.

A rainbow ate him.

The first stroke and he vanished. Then another. Out burst a parade of rainbow rabbits, all excitedly eating pancakes.

The fox
